sourceMovie.title: 22 Jump Street
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto 22 Jump Street (2014) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 21 Jump Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: 21 Jump Street (2012) belongs to the same conversation as 22 Jump Street — both anchor their crime in comedy emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Hot Fuzz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 22 Jump Street's crime stayed with you, Hot Fuzz (2007) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The comedy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Beverly Hills Cop
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where 22 Jump Street excels in crime, Beverly Hills Cop (1984) doubles down on comedy to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Rush Hour 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Rush Hour 2 (2001) is the natural next step after 22 Jump Street: same crime DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and comedy elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Heat
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of 22 Jump Street who respond to its crime will find The Heat (2013) immediately familiar. Both films share a comedy sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Rush Hour 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason 22 Jump Street fans keep landing on Rush Hour 3 (2007) — the crime is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Bad Boys: Ride or Die
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Bad Boys: Ride or Die draws from the same crime well as 22 Jump Street, channeling comedy in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2024 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Hitman's Wife's Bodyguard
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Hitman's Wife's Bodyguard (2021) captures the crime appeal of 22 Jump Street without simply imitating it. The comedy streak running through both films is what puts Hitman's Wife's Bodyguard at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Naked Gun
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime appeal of 22 Jump Street — that funny edge, the comedy — translates cleanly into The Naked Gun (2025). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: The Hard Way
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Hard Way (1991) clearly understood what makes crime work — the same quality that keeps 22 Jump Street rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

faq.question: What should I watch after 22 Jump Street?
faq.answer: Try 21 Jump Street, Hot Fuzz, Beverly Hills Cop—each shares DNA with 22 Jump Street while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is 22 Jump Street?
faq.answer: 22 Jump Street (2014) is best described as Crime, Comedy, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is 22 Jump Street part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Jump Street Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
